LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS NGM BIOPHARMACEUTICALS INC., JEAN-FREDERIC VIRET'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CLARENCE STOKES IS DENIED. NOTWITHSTANDING THE PARTIES' FAILURE TO ENGAGE IN SUFFICIENT MEET AND CONFER, THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO DECIDE THIS MOTION. STOKES HAS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED FACTS SUPPORTING AN AWARD OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST BOTH DEFENDANTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS NGM BIOPHARMACEUTICALS INC., JEAN-FREDERIC VIRET DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances: Loan Dao of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (415.986.5900) for defendants and moving parties; Richard L. Richardson of Siegal & Richardson LLP (510.271.6723) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: overruled in its entirety. Notwithstanding the parties' failure to engage in sufficient meet and confer, the court exercises its discretion to decide this motion. Stokes has adequately alleged facts supporting claims for harassment in violation of FEHA and wrongful termination in violation of public policy and none of his claims are barred by the workers's compensation exclusivity rule. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS NGM BIOPHARMACEUTICALS INC., JEAN-FREDERIC VIRET'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances: Loan Dao of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (415.986.5900) for defendants and moving parties; Richard L. Richardson of Siegal & Richardson LLP (510.271.6723) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Clarence Stokes is denied. Notwithstanding the parties' failure to engage in sufficient meet and confer, the court exercises its discretion to decide this motion. Stokes has adequately alleged facts supporting an award of punitive damages against both defendants. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2025 Continued To Dec-31-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANTS NGM BIOPHARMACEUTICALS INC., AND JEAN-FREDERIC VIRET's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

The issue under section CCP 430.10(e) is, taking the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed as true, does the challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a claim for relief. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) The court liberally construes the complaint pursuant to CCP 452.

Here, Plaintiff Clarence Stokes does not plead sufficient facts, even including those improperly presented in his opposition, to establish any of his causes of action. Mere conclusory statements, such as that Stokes was "discriminated against" and "harassed," are insufficient without providing specific material facts to support each claim. Those statements are legal conclusions, not statements of fact, and will not suffice to state a claim.

Further, the use of a form complaint is improper in this case under CCP 425.12, since the case is not based on personal injury, property damage, wrongful death, unlawful detainer, breach of contract, or fraud.

Additionally, while Defendants are correct that supervisors may not be sued individually under FEHA for alleged discrimination or wrongful termination, supervisors may still be individually liable for harassment. (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 663; Janken v. GM Hughes Electronics (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 55.) Therefore, Defendant Jean-Frederic Vivet cannot be dismissed from the case, since Stokes has the opportunity to plead facts that give rise to a harassment claim against Vivet.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
